title: Understanding the resistance to carbon taxes: Drivers and barriers among the general public and fuel-tax protesters
authors: Jens Ewald, Thomas Sterner, Erik Sterner

tax_mentions.context: Surveyed Swedes rate carbon-tax effectiveness, rank carbon taxes, and judge fairness and revenue recycling/earmarking options.
tax_mentions.notes: Direct acceptability analysis of carbon taxes, including fairness and revenue-use preferences.

tax_mentions.context: Protest sample consists of opponents to petrol/fuel taxes; compared with general public on attitudes toward carbon tax.
tax_mentions.page_or_section: Section 3.1 (Petrol Protest 2.0; petition against petrol taxes)
tax_mentions.notes: Used to define protest group; outcomes focus on carbon-tax attitudes.

excluded_policy_mentions.reason_it_is_not_a_carbon_or_fuel_tax: EU ETS is a permit trading scheme, not a carbon/fuel excise tax.
Subsidies are incentives, not carbon or fuel tax instruments.

inclusion_reason: Uses nationwide and protest surveys in Sweden to directly analyze acceptability of carbon taxes (effectiveness, ranking, fairness, revenue use) and compares determinants between general public and fuel-tax protesters.
